title: NOC 10020 – Insurance, Real Estate, and Financial Brokerage Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Insurance, Real Estate, and Financial Brokerage Managers classified under NOC 10020 fall within one of the most senior and in-demand occupational groups in Canada’s immigration system. These professionals lead operations across insurance, property, mortgage, and securities markets—making them attractive candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).
duties: Professionals in this group may lead one or more of the following business units:
Plan and oversee departments providing automobile, property, life, and other insurance services
Direct operations buying, selling, and leasing residential and commercial properties
Manage departments that arrange financing between borrowers and lenders
Supervise investment trading in stocks, bonds, and portfolios for private or institutional clients
employment_requirements: Occupations in TEER 0 usually require a university degree or college diploma and several years of experience in a related field.
job_outlook: Over the period 2024–2033, Canada is projected to create 19,100 new positions for Insurance, Real Estate, and Financial Brokerage Managers, while approximately 20,700 job seekers may enter the occupation. For internationally trained professionals—particularly applicants from India—this presents encouraging opportunities when paired with a strong immigration strategy.
labour_market_demand: Insurance, Real Estate, and Financial Brokerage Managers are in high demand across Canada’s finance, property, and insurance sectors. Demand at this TEER level expanded rapidly after the pandemic and is expected to remain strong in the years ahead.
Excellent immigration opportunities for overseas professionals
Annual earnings typically range from $82,500 to $158,454, depending on experience, location, and specialization.
Professionals immigrating under NOC 10020 benefit from:
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
benefits: In addition to salary, many employers provide:
Employer contributions to national pension and employment-insurance programs
pathways: At GTR Immigration, we help senior professionals navigate Canada’s immigration system with confidence. Our consultants assess your profile, CRS score, and provincial options to design a customized pathway toward Permanent Residence through Express Entry or PNP streams.
additional_information: 10 – Specialized middle management occupations in administrative services, financial and business services, and communication (except broadcasting)
100 – Specialized middle management occupations in administrative services, financial and business services, and communication (except broadcasting)
1002 – Managers in financial and business services
